channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
item.description: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

item.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘ RAMageddon’
item.description: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.

item.title: Your Agent Success Rate Counts Only the Survivors
item.description: Your agent dashboard says 90% success. It is wrong, and not because the math is sloppy. It is wrong because of which runs it forgot to count. Every run that timed out, got aborted, or is still...

item.title: The Cheapest Open Banking APIs for Small Businesses and Indie Builders in 2026
item.description: If you are a small business, a freelancer, or an indie developer trying to read bank transactions programmatically in Europe, the open banking API market looks deceptively simple on paper and...

item.title: API Testing Tools Worth Knowing in 2026 - Alternatives to Postman
item.description: On March 1, 2026, Postman dropped free team collaboration. The Free plan now supports only a single user - any second teammate accessing a shared workspace breaks the plan. That change sent...

item.title: I built an audit log that catches its own tampering - and proves it at scale, offline
item.description: I built this project for the #H0Hackathon (Hack the Zero Stack with Vercel and AWS Databases). This post covers how I built it using Amazon DynamoDB and Vercel. There's a quiet lie at the center...

item.title: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
The case for letting your UI tell you what to build next
item.description: There's a pattern I keep coming back to that sits somewhere between "chat interface" and "traditional app UI," and I've been trying to figure out whether it's actually interesting or just a cool...

item.title: JavaScript Under the Hood: Scope, Closures, Prototypes &
item.description: A deep dive into the concepts that separate junior devs from engineers who actually understand the runtime. Part 1: Scope and Closures var vs let vs const — It's Not Just Syntax Most devs learn...

item.title: Why Playwright MCP Cost Us 5 More Tokens Than We Expected
item.description: We built an open-source browser automation MCP because we wanted something simple: Observe a webpage once, let an LLM interact with it, then export a Playwright script that actually works. That...

item.title: Stop your agent emailing the wrong recipients
item.description: Here's the failure mode that keeps me up at night about autonomous email agents: not the agent that goes silent, but the one that sends. A model fat-fingers a recipient. It picks up...

item.title: TC39 Type Annotations Proposal in 2026: What JavaScript Developers Need to Know Before It Hits Stage 4
item.description: TC39 Type Annotations Proposal in 2026: What JavaScript Developers Need to Know Before It Hits Stage 4 Most discussions about JavaScript's type future ignore the critical distinction between...

item.title: Building an IAM Service with FastAPI: Refresh Token Families, TOTP MFA, and RBAC
item.description: AuthCore is a production-style Identity and Access Management API built with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, Celery, Alembic, Docker, GitHub Actions, and Trivy. The goal was not another "JWT login"...

item.title: At $499, Apple’s M3-powered iPad Air is a good deal
item.description: Most of Apple’s price increases have gone into effect, resulting in iPads and other products costing hundreds more than they did a few days ago. If last week’s Prime Day sale wasn’t a good time to...

item.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
item.description: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

item.title: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
item.description: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.

item.title: The best July 4th sales we found so far
item.description: July 4th sales are typically a precursor to what we’d see during a mid-July Prime Day, but obviously things are flipped around this year. Last week’s big Prime Day sale is over, yet there are a...

item.title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
item.description: TIDAL's new policy will prevent AI-generated music from making money on its service.

item.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
item.description: WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform. The optional...

item.title: Factoring RSA Keys with Many Zeros
item.description: Interesting research on a new class of weak RSA keys: keys with lots of zeros. It turns out that these keys are out in the wild. The badkeys project is an open-source service that checks public...

item.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
item.description: An illustration depicting a full-stack AI infrastructure against a dark background

item.title: WhatsApp is launching usernames: here’s how to reserve yours
item.description: WhatsApp is introducing a new way to add and chat with contacts, without having to share your phone number. Usernames will launch "later this year," in a move to make the communications platform...

item.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
item.description: A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. In the coming weeks,...

item.title: Dbrand cancels Companion Cube because it didn’t actually ask Valve for permission
item.description: Dbrand announced Monday that it's refunding everyone who bought its Steam Machine Companion Cube, which it said it made "without a license from Valve." Dbrand announced the Portal-themed Steam...

item.title: These camera-free smart glasses made me feel like Tony Stark
item.description: Xgimi, the Chinese company known for its all-in-one smart projectors, is expanding its portfolio with a new line of screen-equipped smart glasses that first debuted at CES 2026. Unlike AR glasses...

item.title: Supreme Court allows firing of FTC commissioners, ends agency independence
item.description: The Supreme Court just placed once-independent agencies more firmly under presidential control. The court ruled in Trump v. Slaughter with a 6-3 vote that President Donald Trump had the authority...

item.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
item.description: The China-aligned espionage group Mustang Panda is running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets, deploying new malware and turning a legitimate cloud service into its...

item.title: Agent confidence on the technical frontier
item.description: Enterprise investment in AI is booming. Gartner is calling 2026 an “inflection year” for organizations to align their AI projects with strategic business objectives. As the pressure to prove ROI...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
item.description: This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open. The noise is not all noise, either....

item.title: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
item.description: The startup, Proception, is taking a unique approach to collecting training data to tackle one of the hardest problems in robotics: hands.
